truth.papers.A74.title: Effect of Al Content on Inclusions in Fe-15Mn-xAl-C Low-Density Steel
truth.papers.A74.paper_scope: Investigate the effect of Al content (1, 3, 5 wt%) on the solidification route, inclusion characteristics, and formation mechanisms in Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steel.

prediction.papers.A74.title: Effect of Al Content on Inclusions in Fe-15Mn-xAl-C Low-Density Steel
prediction.papers.A74.paper_scope: Investigate the effect of Al content on the solidification route, inclusion characteristics (types, number percentage, size distribution), and formation mechanisms in Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steels.

truth.processes.proc_fe15mn_01.description: Laboratory smelting in a tubular furnace under argon atmosphere. Iron powder and ferrous oxalate were heated in an alumina crucible to 1873 K, held for 40 min. Deoxidizer added, then ferromanganese added and held for 20 min, followed by aluminum particles wrapped in iron sheet added and held for 60 min. Finally cooled to room temperature in the furnace. Al mass adjusted per heat.
truth.processes.proc_fe15mn_01.processes_notes: Aluminum particles mass: 11 g for 1#, 28 g for 2#, 48 g for 3#. Iron powder base: 700 g. Ferromanganese: 225 g. Cooling: furnace cool.

prediction.processes.proc_fe15mn_01.description: Laboratory smelting in tubular furnace at 1873 K with addition of 225 g ferromanganese and 48 g aluminum particles, followed by furnace cooling to room temperature.
prediction.processes.proc_fe15mn_01.processes_notes: Operating steps and ferromanganese amount consistent with 1# experiment.

truth.processing_steps.sample_1al_01.processin: Cooled to room temperature in tubular furnace.
truth.processing_steps.sample_3al_01.processin: 28 g aluminum particles wrapped in iron sheet added.
truth.processing_steps.sample_5al_01.processin: 48 g aluminum particles wrapped in iron sheet added.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_1al_01.processin: Sample cooled to room temperature in tubular furnace.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_3al_01.processin: Sample cooled to room temperature in tubular furnace.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_5al_01.processin: Sample cooled to room temperature in tubular furnace.

truth.structures.sample_1al_01.overall_structu: As-cast structure with various inclusions embedded in the matrix.
truth.structures.sample_1al_01.microstructure_: elongated, length > 8 μm; also acts as core in composite inclusions
inner-outer layer composite; Al2O3 core, MnS outer layer
composite; AlN and MnS with clear interfacial relationship
composite; clear interfacial relationship between MnS and TiN
three-layer composite; from inside out: Al2O3, AlN, MnS
Inclusion size distribution: 1-5 μm: 58.4%, 5-10 μm: 32.3%, >10 μm: 9.4% (Figure 5).
single particles, elongated; number percentage decreases with Al content (Figure 4)
single particles; number percentage gradually increases with Al content
single particles; number percentage first increases then decreases with Al
single angular particles; number percentage unchanged with Al
truth.structures.sample_3al_01.overall_structu: As-cast structure with AlN, MnS, TiN and composite inclusions.
truth.structures.sample_3al_01.microstructure_: composite; black Al2O3 core, gray MnS outer layer
composite; close binding relationship between MnS and AlN
three-layer: Al2O3 core, AlN middle, MnS outer (from inside out)
Inclusion size distribution: 1-5 μm: 53.6%, 5-10 μm: 34.6%, >10 μm: 11.8% (Figure 5).
single; number percentage increases compared to 1#
truth.structures.sample_5al_01.overall_structu: As-cast structure with AlN, MnS, TiN and composite inclusions. Al2O3-AlN-MnS not observed.
truth.structures.sample_5al_01.microstructure_: single and multiple particles with polyhedral structures (3D)
composite; Al2O3 at bottom, MnS above
composite; very tight binding, not physical way
Inclusion size distribution: 1-5 μm: 48.2%, 5-10 μm: 46.9%, >10 μm: 4.9% (Figure 5).

prediction.structures.sample_1al_01.overall_structu: FCC austenite matrix with non-metallic inclusions; solidification route L → L+FCC → FCC.
prediction.structures.sample_3al_01.overall_structu: FCC austenite matrix with non-metallic inclusions; solidification route passes through BCC phase (L → L+BCC → L+BCC+FCC → L+FCC → FCC).
prediction.structures.sample_5al_01.overall_structu: FCC austenite matrix with non-metallic inclusions; solidification route passes through BCC phase (L → L+BCC → L+BCC+FCC → BCC+FCC → FCC).

truth.interfaces.idx_0.phase_evolution: Precipitation order: Al2O3 > AlN > MnS > TiN. Later inclusions nucleate heterogeneously on earlier ones, forming composite inclusions. Al2O3, AlN, and TiN provide effective nucleation sites for MnS; Al2O3 also provides nucleation sites for AlN.

prediction.interfaces.sample_1al_01.phase_evolution: Precipitation order of single-particle inclusions: Al2O3 > AlN > MnS > TiN. Later precipitated inclusions can nucleate heterogeneously on earlier formed inclusions, leading to composite inclusions.
prediction.interfaces.sample_3al_01.phase_evolution: Precipitation order of single-particle inclusions: Al2O3 > AlN > MnS > TiN. Later precipitated inclusions can nucleate heterogeneously on earlier formed inclusions, leading to composite inclusions.
prediction.interfaces.sample_5al_01.phase_evolution: Precipitation order of single-particle inclusions: Al2O3 > AlN > MnS > TiN. Later precipitated inclusions can nucleate heterogeneously on earlier formed inclusions, leading to composite inclusions. Al2O3-AlN-MnS not observed in 5% Al sample due to low single-particle Al2O3 content.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Thermodynamic equilibrium calculation using FactSage 8.2 phase diagram module
truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated solidification routes and inclusion precipitation compared with experimental observations.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Calculate element enrichment at solidification front as function of solid fraction and cooling rate
Equilibrium distribution coefficients (k), diffusion coefficients (D), cooling rate (Rc), secondary dendrite arm spacing (λ)
Used to derive actual concentration product Q_AlN, Q_TiN, Q_MnS and compare with equilibrium solubility products. Parameters from references [23-44].
Calculate two-dimensional mismatch degree (δ) to evaluate heterogeneous nucleation potency
Lattice parameters of Al2O3, AlN, MnS, TiN from references [5,48-50]; interatomic spacing along low-index directions
Mismatch values: Al2O3/MnS 8.41%, AlN/MnS 8.94%, TiN/MnS 11.65%, AlN/Al2O3 11.66%. Smaller mismatch (<12%) indicates effective nucleation substrate.
Calculate equilibrium solubility products (K) for AlN, TiN, MnS formation
Standard Gibbs free energy of formation; interaction coefficients (e_i^j, r_i^j) for Al, N, Ti, Mn, S in liquid steel at 1873 K (Table 4); activity coefficients
Equations (1)-(22). log([pctAl][pctN]) vs 1/T relationships derived for each heat.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: FactSage 8.2 phase diagram module calculation for Fe-C-Si-Mn-P-S-Al-O-N-Ti system; Clyne-Kurz segregation model; 2D mismatch theory (Bramfit) for heterogeneous nucleation
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated solidification routes, liquidus/solidus temperatures, and inclusion precipitation fractions compared with experimental SEM/EDS observations and inclusion statistics.
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Calculation of solute segregation at solidification front to obtain actual concentration product Q for inclusion precipitation
Applied to AlN, TiN, and MnS precipitation during solidification; uses equilibrium distribution coefficient k, diffusion coefficient D, solidification time tf, and secondary dendrite arm spacing λ.
Evaluation of heterogeneous nucleation capacity between substrate and nucleation phase inclusions
Mismatch degrees calculated: Al2O3/MnS 8.41%, AlN/MnS 8.94%, TiN/MnS 11.65%, AlN/Al2O3 11.66%.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The N content in steel decreases with the increase of Al content, while the O content slightly increases with the increase of Al content.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The content of Si and Ti is ≈0.32% and 60 ppm in all three steels.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: In 1# sample, Al2O3-MnS composite inclusion number percentage increases with Al, while AlN-MnS and TiN-MnS number percentage decreases. Al2O3-AlN-MnS present in 1# and 2# but not in 3# sample.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The number of inclusions with sizes between 1 and 5 μm accounts for 58.4%, 53.6%, and 48.2% in 1#, 2#, 3# respectively. Between 5 and 10 μm: 32.3%, 34.6%, 46.9%. Higher than 10 μm: 9.4%, 11.8%, 4.9%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Liquidus temperatures (TL): 1# 1702 K, 2# 1740 K, 3# 1796 K. Solidus temperatures (TS): 1588 K, 1560 K, 1532 K.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Solidification route for 1#: L -> L + FCC -> FCC. For 2#: L -> L + BCC -> L + BCC + FCC -> L + FCC -> FCC. For 3#: L -> L + BCC -> L + BCC + FCC -> BCC + FCC -> FCC. Al expands BCC region and narrows FCC region.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Solidification fraction for AlN precipitation: 0.46 (1#), 0.22 (2#), 0.51 (3#). For TiN: 0.89, 0.93, 0.94. For MnS: 0.77, 0.81, 0.85.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Figure 8 illustrates Al and N segregation ratios during solidification; N segregation much higher than Al segregation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Table 4 contains extensive first-order and second-order interaction coefficients at 1873 K used for activity calculations.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Table 5 lists equilibrium distribution coefficients (k) for BCC (δ) and FCC (γ) and diffusion coefficients for Al, N, Mn, S, Ti in both phases.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: The roles of Mn in expanding austenite zone, Al in reducing density (~1.3% per 1% Al) and affecting strain hardening rate and formability are cited from literature but not directly measured here.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The number percentage of single Al2O3 inclusions decreases with increasing Al content, while single AlN increases; single MnS first increases then decreases; single TiN does not change significantly. Composite Al2O3-MnS inclusions increase with Al content, while AlN-MnS and TiN-MnS decrease. Al2O3-AlN-MnS composite inclusions exist in 1# and 2# samples but were not observed in 3# sample (Figure 4).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Inclusion size distribution: 1-5 μm inclusions account for 58.4% (1#), 53.6% (2#), and 48.2% (3#); 5-10 μm account for 32.3%, 34.6%, and 46.9%; >10 μm account for 9.4%, 11.8%, and 4.9%. The number of 1-5 μm inclusions decreases with increasing Al content, while 5-10 μm increases; >10 μm first increases then decreases.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Liquidus temperatures of 1# to 3# samples are 1702 K, 1740 K, and 1796 K, respectively; solidus temperatures are 1588 K, 1560 K, and 1532 K, respectively. Increasing Al raises liquidus and lowers solidus.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Solidification fractions of AlN inclusion precipitation are 0.46 (1#), 0.22 (2#), and 0.51 (3#). For TiN: 0.89, 0.93, and 0.94. For MnS: 0.77, 0.81, and 0.85. Increasing Al content delays precipitation of MnS and TiN, while AlN precipitation is advanced from 1# to 2# and delayed from 2# to 3# due to combined effects of Al and N segregation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Observed inclusion morphologies: elongated Al2O3 (>8 μm), elliptical/single or multiple polyhedral AlN (≈12-23 μm), octahedral MnS (≈10 μm), angular/boot-like/elongated TiN (>30 μm). Composite inclusions exhibit clear interfacial relationships and tight binding between constituent phases (e.g., Al2O3-MnS core-shell, AlN-MnS close binding, TiN-MnS cigarette-bag shape).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The N content in steel decreases from 0.0032% (1#) to 0.0029% (2#) to 0.0021% (3#) with increasing Al content, while O content slightly increases from 0.0005% to 0.0005% to 0.0013%. Si and Ti contents are approximately 0.32% and 60 ppm, respectively.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: The solidification route changes from L→L+FCC→FCC (1#) to routes passing through BCC for 2# and 3# samples. The FCC region narrows and BCC region expands with increasing Al content.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The 2D mismatch degrees of Al2O3/MnS, AlN/MnS, TiN/MnS, and AlN/Al2O3 interfaces are 8.41%, 8.94%, 11.65%, and 11.66%, respectively, indicating that Al2O3, AlN, and TiN can provide heterogeneous nucleation sites for MnS, and Al2O3 can provide nucleation sites for AlN.
